responses.reply: Hi there! I'm your Cybersecurity Awareness Bot. How can I assist you today?
Use strong passwords with uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols. Avoid reusing passwords across sites. Consider using a password manager for better security!
Watch out for suspicious links or urgent requests in emails. Always double-check the sender’s address before clicking anything. Report phishing attempts immediately.
Keep your operating system and apps updated regularly to protect against vulnerabilities and exploits.
I’m here to teach you practical ways to stay safe online—from avoiding scams to securing devices and protecting personal data.
Avoid connecting to public Wi-Fi without a VPN. Public networks are vulnerable. If you must use one, limit sensitive activities like banking or shopping.
Back up important files regularly—preferably both locally and on encrypted cloud storage. Ransomware attacks can lock your data permanently without backups.
Install reliable antivirus software and keep it active. Don’t download attachments or software from unknown sources.
Enable two-factor authentication wherever possible. It adds an extra layer of protection even if someone steals your password.
Review your privacy settings frequently. Limit who can see your posts and personal information. Be mindful of oversharing details like addresses or travel plans.
Encryption protects your data by converting it into unreadable code. Enable device encryption and use messaging apps that support end-to-end encryption.
Never share sensitive personal information unless you’re certain of the recipient’s legitimacy. Monitor your credit reports periodically.
A Virtual Private Network (VPN) hides your IP address and encrypts traffic when browsing. Great for privacy on public connections!
Cookies track your browsing habits. Adjust browser settings to block third-party cookies or clear them regularly for better privacy.
Ransomware locks your files until you pay money. Protect yourself by backing up data and avoiding suspicious downloads or links.
A firewall acts as a barrier between your computer and harmful internet traffic. Most systems come with built-in firewalls—make sure yours is enabled.
Use reputable browsers and enable pop-up blockers. Disable unnecessary plugins and auto-run features to reduce risks.
Educate children about safe internet practices. Use parental controls and discuss risks like chatting with strangers or sharing photos online.
Change default login credentials on smart devices. Update firmware regularly. Place IoT devices behind a separate network when possible.
Report cyberbullying to platforms and authorities. Block offenders and preserve evidence. Speak to someone trusted if affected emotionally.
Thanks for spending time learning about cybersecurity. Stay safe and vigilant online. See you next time!
